description: You use the elements in the string folder to perform string manipulation and substitution operations.

services.name: pub.string:base64Decode
description: WmPublic. Decodes a Base-64 encoded string into a sequence of bytes.

input_parameters.name: string
description: A Base64-encoded String to decode into bytes.

input_parameters.name: encoding
description: - ASCII. This is the default.
- UTF-8

If the Base64-encoded String in string was generated by the pub.string:base64Encode service, the value of the encoding input parameter for pub.string:base64Decode must match the value of the encoding parameter used in the pub.string:base64Encode service invocation.
If the Base64-encoded String in string was obtained from somewhre besides the pub.string:base64Encode service, set the encoding parameter to ASCII.

output_parameters.name: value
description: The sequence of bytes decoded from the Base64-encoded String.

services.name: pub.string:base64Encode
description: WmPublic. Converts a sequence of bytes into a Base64-encoded String.

input_parameters.name: bytes
description: Sequence of bytes to encode into a Base64-encoded String. The input byte[] can be any Java supported charset-encoded byte[].

input_parameters.name: useNewLine
description: - true to retain the line breaks. This is the default.
- false to remove the line breaks.

input_parameters.name: encoding
description: - ASCII. This is the default.
- UTF-8

output_parameters.name: value
description: Base64-encoded String encoded from the sequence of bytes.

usage_notes: By default, the pub.string:base64Encode service inserts line breaks after 76 characters of data, which is not the canonical lexical form expected by implementations such as MTOM. You can use the useNewLine parameter to remove the line breaks. For more information about MTOM implementations, refer to Web Services Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.string:bytesToString
description: WmPublic. Converts a sequence of bytes to a String.

input_parameters.name: bytes
description: Sequence of bytes to convert to a String.

input_parameters.name: encoding
description: To use the default encoding, set encoding to autoDetect.

input_parameters.name: ignoreBOMChars
description: - true to remove the byte order mark (BOM) characters before converting the input sequence of bytes to string, if the byte array contains BOM characters.
- false to include the byte order mark (BOM) characters while converting the input sequence of bytes to string. The default is false.

output_parameters.name: string
description: String representation of the contents of bytes.

services.name: pub.string:compareStrings
description: WmPublic. Performs a case-sensitive comparison of two strings, and indicates whether the strings are identical.

input_parameters.name: inString1
description: Optional. String to compare against inString2. This input variable can be null.

input_parameters.name: inString2
description: Optional. String to compare against inString1. This input variable can be null.

output_parameters.name: isEqual
description: - true indicates that inString1 and inString2 are identical.
- false indicates that inString1 and inString2 are not identical.
Note: If both inString1 and inString2 are null, the service considers the strings to be identical and returns true.

services.name: pub.string:concat
description: WmPublic. Concatenates two strings.

input_parameters.name: inString1
description: String to which you want to concatenate another string.

input_parameters.name: inString2
description: String to concatenate to inString1.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Result of concatenating inString1 with inString2 (inString1 + inString2).

services.name: pub.string:HTMLDecode
description: WmPublic. Replaces HTML character entities with native characters.
Specifically, the service:

input_parameters.name: inString
description: An HTML-encoded String.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Result from decoding the contents of inString. Any HTML character entities that existed in inString will appear as native characters in value.

services.name: pub.string:HTMLEncode
description: WmPublic. Replaces HTML-sensitive characters with equivalent HTML character entities.
Specifically, this service:
These translations are useful when displaying text in an HTML context.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: The character you want to encode in HTML.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Result from encoding the contents of inString. Any HTML-sensitive characters that existed in inString (for example, > or &) will appear as the equivalent HTML character entities in value.

services.name: pub.string:indexOf
description: WmPublic. Returns the index of the first occurrence of a sequence of characters in a string.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: String in which you want to locate a sequence of characters.

input_parameters.name: subString
description: Sequence of characters to locate.

input_parameters.name: fromIndex
description: Optional. Index of inString from which to start the search. If no value is specified, this parameter contains 0 to indicate the beginning of the string.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Index of the first occurrence of subString in inString. If no occurrence is found, this parameter contains -1.

services.name: pub.string:isAlphanumeric
description: WmPublic. Determines whether a string consists entirely of alphanumeric characters (in the ranges A–Z, a–z, or 0–9).

input_parameters.name: inString
description: Optional. String to be checked for alphanumeric characters.

output_parameters.name: isAlphanumeric
description: - true indicates that all the characters in inString are alphanumeric.
- false indicates that not all the characters in inString are alphanumeric. The service returns false if inString is not specified.

services.name: pub.string:isDate
description: WmPublic. Determines whether a string follows a specified date pattern.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: Optional. String to be checked for adherence to the specified date pattern.

input_parameters.name: pattern
description: For more information about the pattern strings that can be specified for the date, see Pattern String Symbols.

output_parameters.name: isDate
description: - true indicates that inString follows the specified date pattern.
- false indicates that inString does not follow the specified date pattern. The service returns false if inString is not specified.

usage_notes: The service returns an error if both inString and pattern are not specified.

You can specify any random string (for example, 111212) as both inString and pattern. The service returns true if the same user-defined string is specified as both inString and pattern. This is because the java.text.SimpleDateFormat class parses the user-defined input string and pattern to a valid date when the particular input values are identical.

You must ensure that the date you provide as inString has the same format as pattern. Because the java.text.SimpleDateFormat class allows you to specify any user-defined input string as a date format, the pub.string:isDate service might return true even if the inString format does not match the specified pattern.

services.name: pub.string:isNullEmptyOrWhitespace
description: WmPublic. Determines if a string is null, empty, or only whitespace.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: Optional. String to be checked.

input_parameters.name: ifPresent
description: - If the value is set to true, the service checks whether inString is present or not, and only then proceeds with validation of the input.
- If the value is set to false, the service throws an exception when inString is absent. Else, the service proceeds to validate the input.

output_parameters.name: isNullEmptyOrWhitespace
description: - true indicates that inString has a null value, is empty, or is only whitespace.
- false indicates that inString is not null, not empty, or is not only whitespace.

usage_notes: pub.string:isNullEmptyOrWhitespace replaces pub.string:isNullOrBlank which is deprecated.

services.name: pub.string:isNullOrBlank
description: WmPublic. Deprecated - Use pub.string:isNullEmptyOrWhitespace.
Checks a string to determine if it has a null or empty value.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: Optional. String to be checked.

input_parameters.name: ifPresent
description: - If the value is set to true, the service checks whether inString is present or not, and only then proceeds with validation of the input.
- If the value is set to false, the service throws an exception when inString is absent. Else, the service proceeds to validate the input.

output_parameters.name: isNullorBlank
description: - true indicates that inString has either a null value or an empty value.
- false indicates that inString contains a value that is not null or not empty.

services.name: pub.string:isNumber
description: WmPublic. Determines whether the contents of a string can be converted to a float value.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: Optional. String to be checked for conversion to float.

output_parameters.name: isNumber
description: - true indicates that inString can be converted to a float value.
- false indicates that inString cannot be converted to a float value. The service returns false if inString is not specified.

services.name: pub.string:length
description: WmPublic. Returns the length of a string.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: String whose length you want to discover.

input_parameters.name: encoding
description: The pub.string:length service ends with an exception if JVM does not recognize the specified encoding.

output_parameters.name: value
description: The number of characters in inString.

services.name: pub.string:lookupDictionary
description: WmPublic. Looks up a given key in a hash table and returns the string to which that key is mapped.

input_parameters.name: hashtable
description: Hash table that uses String objects for keys and values.

input_parameters.name: key
description: Note: The key is case sensitive.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Value of the string to which key is mapped. If the requested key in hashtable is null or if key is not mapped to any value in hashtable, the service returns null.

services.name: pub.string:lookupTable
description: WmPublic. Locates a key in a String Table and returns the string to which that key is mapped.

input_parameters.name: lookupTable
description: A multi-row, multi-column string table in which to search.

input_parameters.name: keyColumnIndex
description: Index of the "key" column. Default is 0.

input_parameters.name: valueColumnIndex
description: Index of the "value" column. Default is 1.

input_parameters.name: key
description: Note: The key is case sensitive.

input_parameters.name: ignoreCase
description: - true to perform a case-insensitive search.
- false to perform a case-sensitive search. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: useRegex
description: Note: The regular expressions in the table should not include slashes. For example, use hello.*, not /hello.*/.
Set to:
- true to interpret the key column values in the table as regular expressions.
- false to interpret the key column values in the table as literal values (that is, not regular expressions). This is the default.

input_parameters.name: regexTimeout
description: Optional. Specifies the maximum execution time in milliseconds for regex matching. Integration Server supports the Perl5 (Apache ORO) and Java Regex engines for regex matching. When regexTimeout is set, Integration Server uses Java Regex regardless of the configured regex engine. If not set, the configured regex engine (Perl5 or Java Regex) is used. By default, the parameter value is blank.

output_parameters.name: value
description: First value in the "value" column whose key matches key. If no match is found, this parameter is null.

services.name: pub.string:makeString
description: WmPublic. Builds a single string by concatenating the elements of a String List.

input_parameters.name: elementList
description: Strings to concatenate.

input_parameters.name: separator
description: String to insert between each non-null element in elementList.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Result from concatenating the strings in elementList. Strings are separated by the characters specified in separator.

services.name: pub.string:messageFormat
description: WmPublic. Formats an array of strings into a given message pattern.

input_parameters.name: pattern
description: Test results: {0} items passed, {1} items failed.
Note: Do not use any characters except digits for n.

input_parameters.name: argumentList
description: Optional. List of strings to use to populate pattern. If argumentList is not supplied, the service will not replace placeholders in pattern with actual values.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Result from substituting argumentList into pattern. If pattern is empty or null, this parameter is null.

usage_notes: - This service uses the pattern syntax and formatting rules defined by java.text.MessageFormat. See the Java documentation to learn how to use placeholders and escape special characters such as single quotes.

- The service handles single quotes in the following ways: It preserves a literal single quote between two letters (for example, don't) by replacing it with two single quotes ('') during processing. It does not replace two single quotes between two letters with four single quotes. It replaces placeholders correctly when the pattern uses single quotes, as long as the pattern follows java.text.MessageFormat syntax.

input_parameters.name: num
description: The number to format.

input_parameters.name: pattern
description: A pattern string that describes the way in which num is to be formatted:

output_parameters.name: value
description: num formatted according to pattern. If pattern is an empty (not null) string, the default pattern of comma separators is used and the number of digits after the decimal point remains unchanged.

services.name: pub.string:objectToString
description: WmPublic. Converts an object to string representation using the Java toString() method of the object.

input_parameters.name: object
description: The object to be converted to string representation.

output_parameters.name: string
description: String representation of the input object converted using the Java toString() method of the object.

services.name: pub.string:padLeft
description: WmPublic. Pads a string to a specified length by adding pad characters to the beginning of the string.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: String that you want to pad.

input_parameters.name: padString
description: Characters to use to pad inString.

input_parameters.name: length
description: Total length of the resulting string, including pad characters.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Contents of inString preceded by as many pad characters as needed so that the total length of the string equals length.

usage_notes: If padString is longer than one character and does not fit exactly into the resulting string, the beginning of padString is aligned with the beginning of the resulting string. For example, suppose inString equals shipped and padString equals x9y.
7: shipped
10: x9yshipped
12: x9x9yshipped
If inString is longer than length characters, only the last length characters from inString are returned. For example, if inString equals acct1234 and length equals 4, value will contain 1234.

services.name: pub.string:padRight
description: WmPublic. Pads a string to a specified length by adding pad characters to the end of the string.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: String that you want to pad.

input_parameters.name: padString
description: Characters to use to pad inString.

input_parameters.name: length
description: Total length of the resulting string, including pad characters.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Contents of inString followed by as many pad characters as needed so that the total length of the string equals length.

usage_notes: If padString is longer than one character and does not fit exactly into the resulting string, the end of padString is aligned with the end of the resulting string. For example, suppose inString equals shipped and padString equals x9y.
7: shipped
10: shippedx9y
12: shippedx9y9y
If inString is longer than length characters, only the first length characters from inString are returned. For example, if inString equals 1234acct and length equals 4, value will contain 1234.

services.name: pub.string:replace
description: WmPublic. Replaces all occurrences of a specified substring with a substitute string.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: String containing the substring to replace.

input_parameters.name: searchString
description: Substring to replace within inString.

input_parameters.name: replaceString
description: Character sequence that will replace searchString. If this parameter is null or empty, the service removes all occurrences of searchString from inString.

input_parameters.name: useRegex
description: Set to:
- true to indicate that searchString is a regular expression.
- false to indicate that searchString is not a regular expression. This is the default.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Contents of inString with replacements made.

services.name: pub.string:stringToBytes
description: WmPublic. Converts a string to a byte array.

input_parameters.name: string
description: String to convert to a byte[ ].

input_parameters.name: encoding
description: To use the default encoding, set this value to autoDetect. If you specify an unsupported encoding, an exception will be thrown.

output_parameters.name: bytes
description: Contents of string represented as a byte[ ].

services.name: pub.string:substitutePipelineVariables
description: WmPublic. Replaces a pipeline variable with its corresponding value.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: Optional. String containing the pipeline variable to replace. Specify the name of the pipeline variable between the % symbols (for example, %phone% or %array_name[n]%).

output_parameters.name: value
description: Contents of inString with the pipeline variable replaced.

usage_notes: The service returns an error if inString is not specified.
If inString does not contain a pipeline variable (a variable or an array element) between the % symbols, the service does not perform any variable substitution from the pipeline.

If you want to include the % symbol in the output, you can specify it as \% in inString. To specify the value of the pipeline variable as a percentage in the output, append \% after the variable name in inString. For example, suppose a pipeline variable revenueIncreasePercent has a value of 100.
%revenueIncreasePercent%\%: 100%
The service cannot be used for substitution of global variables.

services.name: pub.string:substring
description: WmPublic. Returns a substring of a given string.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: String from which to extract a substring.

input_parameters.name: beginIndex
description: Beginning index of the substring to extract (inclusive).

input_parameters.name: endIndex
description: Ending index of the substring to extract (exclusive). If this parameter is null or empty, the substring will extend to the end of inString.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Substring from beginIndex and extending to the character at endIndex - 1.

services.name: pub.string:tokenize
description: WmPublic. Tokenizes a string using specified delimiter characters and generates a String List from the resulting tokens.
This service does not return delimiters as tokens.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: String you want to tokenize (that is, break into delimited chunks).

input_parameters.name: delim
description: Delimiter characters. If null or empty, the service uses the default delimiters \t\n\r, where t, n, and r represent the white space characters tab, new line, and carriage return).

input_parameters.name: useRegex
description: - If the value is set to true, Integration Server supports recognizing the delimiter character set for the delim parameter as a regular expression.
- If the value is set to false, Integration Server considers the delimiter character set for the delim parameter as an individual character. This is the default.

output_parameters.name: valueList
description: Strings containing the tokens extracted from inString.

services.name: pub.string:toLower
description: WmPublic. Converts all characters in a given string to lowercase.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: String to convert.

input_parameters.name: language
description: Optional. Lowercase, two-letter ISO-639 code. If this parameter is null, the system default is used.

input_parameters.name: country
description: Optional. Uppercase, two-letter ISO-3166 code. If this parameter is null, the system default is used.

input_parameters.name: variant
description: Optional. Vendor and browser-specific code. If null, this parameter is ignored.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Contents of inString, with all uppercase characters converted to lowercase.

services.name: pub.string:toUpper
description: WmPublic. Converts all characters in a given string to uppercase.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: String to convert.

input_parameters.name: language
description: Optional. Lowercase, two-letter ISO-639 code. If this parameter is null, the system default is used.

input_parameters.name: country
description: Optional. Uppercase, two-letter ISO-3166 code. If this parameter is null, the system default is used.

input_parameters.name: variant
description: Optional. Vendor and browser-specific code. If null, this parameter is ignored.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Contents of inString, with all lowercase characters converted to uppercase.

services.name: pub.string:trim
description: WmPublic. Trims leading white space, trailing white space, or both from a given string.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: String to trim.

input_parameters.name: type
description: Set to:
- left to strip the leading white space at the start of the input string.
- right to strip the trailing white space at the end of the input string.
- both to strip the leading and trailing white space from the input string. This is the default.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Contents of inString with white space trimmed based on the value specified for the type input parameter.

services.name: pub.string:URLDecode
description: WmPublic. Decodes a URL-encoded string.

input_parameters.name: inString
description: URL-encoded string to decode.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Result from decoding inString. If inString contained plus (+) signs, they will appear in value as spaces. If inString contained %hex encoded characters, they will appear in value as the appropriate native character.

services.name: pub.string:URLEncode
description: WmPublic. URL-encodes a string.
Encodes characters the same way that data posted from a WWW form is encoded (that is, the application/x-www-form-urlencoded MIME type).

input_parameters.name: inString
description: String to URL-encode.

output_parameters.name: value
description: Result from URL-encoding inString. If inString contained non-alphanumeric characters (except -_.*@), they will appear in value as their URL-encoded equivalents (% followed by a two-digit hex code). If inString contained spaces, they will appear in value as plus (+) signs.
